title: Asynchronous Architecture Decisions for Go Services · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record with one stale generation, blocked notification, goroutine leak, acknowledgement, route, or effect defect; locate cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Temporal Coupling Decision
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go messaging team handles case 6418 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Inspect the Temporal Coupling Decision trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Broker Fit Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-go-broker-fit-boundary-1mz7qa" that uses case 9242 to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqp091-go, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, amqp091-go 1.12.0, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production gates.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Broker Fit Boundary has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Broker Fit Boundary, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Broker Fit Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Broker Fit Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go messaging team handles case 8819 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Broker Fit Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: RabbitMQ queues are a durable event log and should retain all history indefinitely. In Go, adding a channel or broker wrapper does not remove temporal coupling.

options.text: Declare RabbitMQ and Go outcome, topology, ownership, identity, and byte, time, attempt, and concurrency budgets for the debug Broker Fit Boundary case
Predict RabbitMQ and Go publish, route, custody, delivery, effect, failure, cancellation, and recovery outcomes for the debug Broker Fit Boundary case
Run the smallest deterministic RabbitMQ and Go messaging model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the debug Broker Fit Boundary case
Inject one changed RabbitMQ and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-broker transfer limits for the debug Broker Fit Boundary case

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Broker Fit Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Broker Fit Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Go messaging team handles case 1438 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Broker Fit Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Broker Fit Boundary, keep Broker Fit Boundary connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
